summary: n=10 fully-blind alias-safety A/B on stock Haven (Haiku + Opus, raw vs site_architecture)

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No route, alias, or entity claims this path. Safe for use as new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route, alias, or entity claims this path. Safe for use as new node URL alias.

/blog.reason: Canvas page entity (ID 2, title: Blog) is served at this path. Cannot be used as new node alias.

/search.reason: Enabled Views page route (search view) is served at this path. Cannot be used as new node alias.

/admin/content/files.reason: Associated view is disabled. No active route at this path. Safe for use as new node URL alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Associated view is disabled. No active route at this path. Safe for use as new node URL alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity currently responds to this path.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity currently responds to this path.

/search.reason: Claimed by Views page display search:page (Search view); creating a node alias here would shadow the view.

/admin/content/files.reason: Latent claim: disabled view files:page_1 declares this path. Collision risk if view is enabled.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Latent claim: disabled view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview declares this path. Collision risk if view is enabled.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No route, no path alias, and no redirect claim this path. router.no_access_checks throws ResourceNotFoundException, path_alias.manager returns the path unchanged, and no path_alias/redirect entity references it. Free to use.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, or redirect. Note the Content Moderation view lives at /admin/content/moderated (not /moderated-content), so despite the similar name there is no collision. Free to use.

/blog.reason: Already claimed. There is a path alias /blog -> /page/2 and the router resolves it to entity.canvas_page.canonical for an existing canvas_page entity (id=2, label 'Blog'). A new node alias here would collide with that page.

/search.reason: Already claimed by the enabled 'search' view: its page display is mounted at /search and the router resolves /search to route view.search.page. The path is live.

/admin/content/files.reason: Reserved. No live route currently because the 'files' view is DISABLED, but the path /admin/content/files is declared in that view's page_1 display config and sits under the reserved /admin namespace; it would be reclaimed as soon as the view is enabled. Not safe for a node alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Reserved. Currently no live route because the 'scheduler_scheduled_media' view is DISABLED, but the path /admin/content/media/scheduled is declared in that view's overview display config and is under the reserved /admin namespace; it would be reclaimed when the view is enabled. Not safe for a node alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path (confirmed by path-owner tool, path_alias.manager returns the path unchanged, and 0 matching routes). An agent may safely create a node with this URL alias here.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no alias, no matching route, and no view declares this path. The enabled core 'Moderated content' view (moderated_content:moderated_content) lives at the different path /admin/content/moderated, not /moderated-content, so there is no collision.

/blog.reason: Already claimed: it is the canonical URL alias of canvas_page entity 2 ('Blog'), aliasing internal path /page/2. Creating a node with this alias would shadow/collide with that existing page.

/search.reason: Already claimed by the enabled Views page display search:page ('Search', route view.search.page), a computed listing over search_api_index_content. A node alias here would shadow the view and hijack the page.

/admin/content/files.reason: Latent collision risk: no enabled route responds now, but the DISABLED view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares this exact path. If that view is ever enabled it will collide with anything created here. It is also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Latent collision risk: no enabled route responds now, but the DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this exact path. If that view is ever enabled it will collide with anything created here. It is also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No router route, no path_alias row, no View page display, and no menu link claim this path. Fully available as a new node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route, no alias, and no menu link. The enabled moderated_content View sits at /admin/content/moderated (a different path), so it does not conflict with /moderated-content.

/blog.reason: Claimed by an active URL alias (path_alias id=23) that maps /blog to internal /page/2, a real canvas_page node (route entity.canvas_page.canonical, canvas_page #2).

/search.reason: Claimed by an enabled View page display: view 'search' display 'page' registers route view.search.page at /search.

/admin/content/files.reason: Defined by View 'files' (display page_1) at this exact path. The view is currently DISABLED so no route is registered today, but the path is reserved in config and would be reclaimed if the view is re-enabled. It is also in the reserved /admin namespace, which is unsuitable for a public node alias.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Defined by View 'scheduler_scheduled_media' (display 'overview') at this exact path. The view is currently DISABLED so no route exists today, but the path is reserved in config and would be reclaimed if the view is re-enabled. It is also in the reserved /admin namespace, unsuitable for a public node alias.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path (confirmed: router NO MATCH, 0 alias rows). Safe to use as a new node's URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, or entity, and no latent/disabled-view claims (confirmed: router NO MATCH, 0 alias rows). Safe to use as a new node's URL alias.

/blog.reason: Already claimed. It is the canonical URL of canvas_page entity 2 ('Blog'); the alias maps /blog to internal /page/2 and matches route entity.canvas_page.canonical. A new node here would conflict with that entity.

/search.reason: Already claimed by the enabled Views page display search:page (route view.search.page, config views.view.search), a search listing over search_api_index_content. A node alias at /search would shadow and hijack the view.

/admin/content/files.reason: Risky / not safe. No enabled route responds today, but the DISABLED view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares this path; if it is ever enabled it collides with whatever is created here. Also sits in the reserved /admin namespace.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Risky / not safe. No enabled route responds today, but the DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this path; enabling it would collide. Also sits in the reserved /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No path alias, no route (router.no_access_checks throws ResourceNotFoundException), no view display path, and no redirect entity claims it. Free to use as a node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No alias, no route, no redirect, and not a view path. The Content Moderation 'Moderated content' view is at /admin/content/moderated (different path), so /moderated-content is unclaimed.

/blog.reason: Already claimed by a path alias: /blog -> /page/2, which resolves to route entity.canvas_page.canonical (an existing Canvas page node). Using it as a new node's alias would collide. (Separately, /blog/feed is an RSS view, but the conflict is the /blog alias itself.)

/search.reason: Claimed by the core 'search' view page display (route view.search.page, view search:page) bound to path /search.

/admin/content/files.reason: No route, no alias, no redirect, and no view display at this path; no routes exist under the /admin/content/files prefix. Technically unclaimed. Note it lives in the /admin/content/ administrative namespace, so it is an unusual/ill-advised place for a content node alias, but nothing currently claims the exact path.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No route, no alias, no redirect, and no view at this exact path. Routes under /admin/content/media/ are bulk-upload, bulk-upload/{media_type}, and the media collection at /admin/content/media; none match /scheduled. Scheduler's scheduled-content view is at /admin/content/scheduled (different path). Unclaimed, though it sits in the /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed. No route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path; no negative contracts or latent claims. Safe to use as a new node's URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed. No route, alias, view, or entity responds to this path; no negative contracts or latent claims. Safe to use as a new node's URL alias.

/blog.reason: Claimed: it is the URL alias (internal /page/2) of the canonical page of canvas_page entity 2 (label "Blog"). Creating another surface here would collide. Edit that entity instead.

/search.reason: Claimed by the Views page display search:page (view config views.view.search, route view.search.page), a computed listing over search_api_index_content. A node alias of /search would shadow the view and hijack the page.

/admin/content/files.reason: Risky. No enabled route responds now, but a DISABLED view (files:page_1, config views.view.files) declares this exact path. If that view is enabled it would collide with any surface created here. Also lives under the reserved /admin namespace.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Risky. No enabled route responds now, but a DISABLED view (scheduler_scheduled_media:overview, config views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this exact path. If enabled it would collide. Also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No route, no path alias, and no view claims this path. Free to use as a node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route, no alias, no view page display at this path. (The moderated_content admin view is at /admin/content/moderated, not here.) Free to use.

/blog.reason: Already claimed: path_alias entity id 23 maps /blog -> /page/2 (canvas_page node 2, 'Blog'), resolving to route entity.canvas_page.canonical.

/search.reason: Claimed by a live route view.search.page (path /search) from the enabled 'search' view page display.

/admin/content/files.reason: Latent collision. No live route today because view 'files' (page_1) is disabled, but that view's page display config still claims path /admin/content/files. Re-enabling the Files view would collide with a node alias here.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Latent collision. No live route today because view 'scheduler_scheduled_media' (overview) is disabled, but its page display config still claims path /admin/content/media/scheduled. Enabling scheduler media would collide with a node alias here.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed. site-architecture:path-owner reports claimed=false; live router returns NO ROUTE and the path_alias manager returns the path unchanged (no alias). No route, view, or entity responds. Safe to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed. content_moderation IS enabled, but its moderated-content listing route (content_moderation.admin_moderated_content) lives at /admin/content/moderated, NOT at /moderated-content. The live router returns NO ROUTE for /moderated-content and no alias exists. Safe.

/blog.reason: Claimed. It is an existing URL alias (internal /page/2) and the canonical page of canvas_page entity 2 ("Blog", route entity.canvas_page.canonical). A new node alias here would collide with / shadow that page.

/search.reason: Claimed by a Views page display: search:page (route view.search.page, config views.view.search), a computed search listing over search_api_index_content. A node alias here would shadow and hijack the view.

/admin/content/files.reason: Risky. No enabled route responds right now, but a DISABLED view files:page_1 (views.view.files) declares this exact path; enabling it would collide. Also an /admin/... namespace is inappropriate for a content node alias. Avoid.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: Risky. No enabled route responds right now, but a DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media) declares this exact path; enabling it (Scheduler media integration) would collide. Also an /admin/... namespace is inappropriate for a content node alias. Avoid.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No path_alias row and no router match (ResourceNotFoundException; 0 routes by exact pattern). Not claimed by any alias or View display. Free to use as a node alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No path_alias row and no route match. The moderated-content View lives at /admin/content/moderated, not /moderated-content, so this exact path is unclaimed despite the suggestive name. Free to use.

/blog.reason: Already claimed by a URL alias: path_alias id=23 maps /blog -> internal /page/2, which resolves to route entity.canvas_page.canonical (a Canvas page node). Using it for a new node would collide with this existing alias. (Note: /blog/feed is also a View display, projects:rss.)

/search.reason: Already claimed by a route: the 'search' View's page display owns /search (route view.search.page, 1 route by exact pattern). Not available for a new node alias.

/admin/content/files.reason: No route (0 routes by exact pattern; the file module's admin file-listing View is not present/enabled here) and no path_alias row. The path is technically unclaimed and free as an alias, though placing public node content under /admin is unconventional.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No route and no path_alias row; this exact path is unclaimed. Nearby paths are owned by Views (/admin/content/media = media:media_page_list, /admin/content/scheduled = scheduler_scheduled_content:overview, /user/%user/scheduled) but none claim /admin/content/media/scheduled. Free as an alias, though the /admin prefix is unconventional for a node.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed. path-owner reports claimed=false with no route, alias, view, or entity responding to this path. Free to use as a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed. path-owner reports claimed=false with no route, alias, view, or entity responding to this path. Free to use as a new node URL alias.

/blog.reason: Already claimed: it is the canonical URL alias for canvas_page entity 2 ("Blog", internal path /canvas_page/2, published). A new node alias here would collide with that existing entity page.

/search.reason: Already claimed by the enabled Views page display search:page (route view.search.page, config views.view.search), a Search API listing over search_api_index_content. A node alias here would shadow/hijack the view.

/admin/content/files.reason: No enabled route currently responds, but the DISABLED view files:page_1 (config views.view.files, confirmed status=false) declares this path. Claiming it risks a collision if that view is ever enabled. Also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No enabled route currently responds, but the DISABLED view scheduler_scheduled_media:overview (config views.view.scheduler_scheduled_media, confirmed status=false) declares this path. Claiming it risks a collision if that view is ever enabled. Also under the reserved /admin namespace.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: No matching route (ResourceNotFoundException), no path alias (path_alias table has 0 rows for it), and no redirect. The path is unclaimed and free to use as a new node's URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: No route, no path alias, no redirect. Note a distinct admin path /admin/content/moderated exists (moderated_content view), but the candidate /moderated-content itself is unclaimed and safe. Watch the near-collision in naming only.

/blog.reason: Already claimed. /blog is a URL alias (path_alias id=23, status=1) pointing to internal /page/2, which is the canvas_page entity id=2 labeled 'Blog' (route entity.canvas_page.canonical). Using it for a new node would collide with this existing alias.

/search.reason: Already claimed by a route. /search matches view.search.page (the search view's page display). A node alias here would be shadowed by the existing route, so it is not safe.

/admin/content/files.reason: No exact route (ResourceNotFoundException), no alias, no redirect for this exact path. Technically unclaimed. Caveat: it sits under the /admin namespace where /admin/content (system.admin_content) and /admin/content/media (entity.media.collection) are real routes; the exact path /admin/content/files is free, but placing a public node under /admin is unconventional.

/admin/content/media/scheduled.reason: No exact route (ResourceNotFoundException), no alias, no redirect for this exact path. Note /admin/content/media is the media collection route and a scheduler 'scheduled content' view exists at /admin/content/scheduled, but the exact candidate /admin/content/media/scheduled is unclaimed. Caveat: it lives under the /admin namespace, so it is technically free but an unusual location for a content node.

/this-is-free-xyz.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, entity, or redirect responds to this path. Safe for a new node URL alias.

/moderated-content.reason: Unclaimed: no route, alias, view, entity, or redirect responds. (Core's moderated_content view is at /admin/content/moderated, not here.) Safe for a new node URL alias.

/blog.reason: Claimed. /blog is an existing URL alias (-> /canvas_page/2), the canonical page of canvas_page entity 2 ('Blog'). A new node alias here would shadow/collide with it.

/search.reason: Claimed by the Views page display search:page (route view.search.page), a computed search_api listing. A node alias at /search would shadow the view and hijack the page.
